deepresearch_plan.stop_condition: primary ICP, anti-ICP, claim ledger, and taste proposal are specific enough to review

cited.claim_supported: the product targets governed AI coding workflows

claim_ledger.claim: Best-fit users need inspectable AI coding work rather than fast unverified output.
claim_ledger.label: repo-derived
claim_ledger.evidence: README and taste.vision emphasize research-backed, spec-backed, verifiable work.
claim_ledger.taste_impact: Preserve correctness, verification, and traceability as dominant product taste.

claim_ledger.claim: The ICP is likely technical enough to review artifacts but wants the harness to reduce coordination burden.
claim_ledger.label: inference
claim_ledger.evidence: Derived from repo command surfaces and workflow artifacts.
claim_ledger.taste_impact: Use provisional language in audience and experience sections.

primary.segment: solo or small-team builders shipping real software with AI agents
primary.job_to_be_done: turn ambiguous coding work into researched, planned, implemented, verified changes with an audit trail
pain_points: parallel work creates merge and trust risk
trigger_events: preparing to sell or demo a product
primary.buyer_context: technical founder, CTO, or senior builder with authority over local tooling
primary.adoption_channel: Claude Code CLI project skills and one-command install
disqualifiers: projects that cannot tolerate local agent tooling

secondary.segment: consultants packaging governed AI workflows for clients
secondary.difference: needs clearer operator-facing docs and proof artifacts

anti_icp: users seeking the shortest possible prompt-to-code path with no audit trail

taste_md_changes.change: Make operator confidence and low-friction evidence review explicit for technical founders.

taste_vision_changes.change: Name solo builders, CTOs, and consultants using AI agents on real repos.

changed_line_trace.label: repo-derived
changed_line_trace.rationale: Audience copy should reflect the highest-value users already implied by repo behavior.

likely_mistakes: overfitting the whole kernel to a temporary feature
treating inferred buyer context as fact
